content.problemStatement:
ShareChatland is a grid with NNN rows (numbered 000 through NтИТ1N-1NтИТ1) and MMM columns (numbered 000 through MтИТ1M-1MтИТ1). There is a prince and a princess in this land; initially, the prince is in cell (0,0)(0, 0)(0,0) and the princess is in cell (NтИТ1,MтИТ1)(N-1, M-1)(NтИТ1,MтИТ1).

The prince and princess want to meet. The prince has a visibility radius R1R_1R1тАЛ. In one step, he can move to any cell such that the Manhattan distance between this cell and his current cell is тЙдR1\le R_1тЙдR1тАЛ (staying in the same cell is also possible). The princess has a visibility radius R2R_2R2тАЛ and moves in a similar way.

There are also KKK evil minions in the grid. A minion is initially located at (x1,y1)(x_1, y_1)(x1тАЛ,y1тАЛ) and moves to (x2,y2)(x_2, y_2)(x2тАЛ,y2тАЛ); it is guaranteed that these cells are either in the same row or in the same column, i.e. x1=x2x_1 = x_2x1тАЛ=x2тАЛ or y1=y2y_1 = y_2y1тАЛ=y2тАЛ. In one step, a minion moves to the adjacent cell that's closest to its target cell. When it reaches (x2,y2)(x_2, y_2)(x2тАЛ,y2тАЛ), it starts moving to (x1,y1)(x_1, y_1)(x1тАЛ,y1тАЛ), then back to (x2,y2)(x_2, y_2)(x2тАЛ,y2тАЛ) and so on.

The prince and princess can see minions, but they cannot see each other. Therefore, in each step, the prince and princess both uniformly randomly choose cells within their visibility radii that are not occupied by minions after this step and move to these cells. Simultaneously, the minions make their moves. (That means it is allowed for the prince or princess and a minion to swap places in one step.) If the prince or princess does not have any cell to move to, then he or she dies.

Find the probability that the prince and princess meet (are located in the same cell) after exactly ttt steps. This probability can be expressed as a fraction P/QP/QP/Q, where PPP and QQQ are coprime integers; you should compute PтЛЕQтИТ1P \cdot Q^{-1}PтЛЕQтИТ1 modulo 109+710^9+7109+7, where QтИТ1Q^{-1}QтИТ1 is the multiplicative inverse of QQQ modulo 109+710^9+7109+7 (it can be proven that this number exists and is unique).
constraints: for each minion, either x1=x2x_1 = x_2x1тАЛ=x2тАЛ or y1=y2y_1 = y_2y1тАЛ=y2тАЛ
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains six space-separated integers NNN, MMM, R1R_1R1тАЛ, R2R_2R2тАЛ, KKK and ttt.
Each of the following KKK lines contains four space-separated integers x1x_1x1тАЛ, y1y_1y1тАЛ, x2x_2x2тАЛ and y2y_2y2тАЛ describing a minion.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing one integer тАФ the required probability as PтЛЕQтИТ1P \cdot Q^{-1}PтЛЕQтИТ1 modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
There is one minion, initially in cell (1,0)(1, 0)(1,0). This minion is moving back and forth between cells (1,0)(1, 0)(1,0) and (1,1)(1, 1)(1,1). That is, after 111 step, its position is (1,1)(1, 1)(1,1), after 222 steps, it is (1,0)(1, 0)(1,0) and so on.
We are interested in the situation after 111 step. Then, the prince can be at (0,0)(0, 0)(0,0), (1,0)(1, 0)(1,0) or (0,1)(0, 1)(0,1), and the princess can be at (0,1)(0, 1)(0,1) or (1,0)(1, 0)(1,0). Note that she cannot choose to stay at (1,1)(1, 1)(1,1), since the minion is there at that time. The probability of them meeting is 1/31/31/3, which is 333333336333333336333333336 modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.
